buckets.cannotEditBucketOwnerAdmin: You cannot edit the admin settings for the owner of a bucket.
buckets.bucketPermissionsInfo: Bucket permissions apply to this bucket and nested buckets.
buckets.addAdminDescription: Select the permissions you want to assign to the new admin. Pressing "Add admin" creates a link you can send to them; when they open it, they can accept or decline the invitation.
buckets.inviteLinkCopy: Share this link. When they open it, they can accept or decline.
buckets.roleDescriptionBucketFull: Grants full create, read, update, and delete permissions for buckets, messages, and admins.
buckets.roleDescriptionBucketRead: Grants read-only permissions for buckets and messages.
buckets.roleDescriptionCustomRole: Use a custom role when you need permissions that do not match a predefined option.

howToCreators.title: How-To for Creators
howToCreators.intro: This guide explains the shortest path to receive MB1-style boost messages from compatible apps without extra implementation detail.
howToCreators.step1Body: Create a root bucket for your show/channel and add item-level buckets when you need per-episode tracking.
howToCreators.step2Title: 2) Add your metaBoost tag to RSS
howToCreators.step2Body: Put your MB1 endpoint URL into the RSS channel podcast:metaBoost tag so supporting apps know where to send boost messages.
howToCreators.step3Body: Verify RSS setup in MetaBoost, then choose whether your message views are public or private before sharing links.
howToCreators.step4Body: Messages and payments are related but separate. A message can exist without confirmed settlement, and settlement can happen without message delivery.
howToCreators.policyMiddle: for payment and refund disclaimers. Developer integration details are in

howToDevelopers.title: How-To for Developers
howToDevelopers.intro: This guide covers the MB1 integration flow your app needs to send and confirm boost messages with MetaBoost.
howToDevelopers.step1Body: Call the MB1 capability endpoint first and respect message_char_limit, terms_of_service_url, and any public message links returned by the server.
howToDevelopers.step2Body: Send boost payloads to the MB1 ingest endpoint with required fields (currency, amount, app_name, feed_guid, feed_title) plus optional fields such as amount_unit, sender_name, and message.
howToDevelopers.step3Body: Use the confirm-payment endpoint after your payment flow completes. Message acceptance is not itself proof that a payment was settled.
howToDevelopers.step4Body: Use the public messages endpoints for bucket, channel, or item views when the creator has enabled public visibility.
howToDevelopers.policyMiddle: for payment/refund disclaimers and responsibilities between sender, creator, and payment service provider.
